Klasse GUIBoard

java.lang.Object
java.awt.Component
java.awt.Container
javax.swing.JComponent
javax.swing.JPanel
view.GUIBoard
Alle implementierten Schnittstellen:
ImageObserver, MenuContainer, Serializable, Accessible
public class GUIBoard extends JPanel
This class is responsible for rendering and managing the graphical representation of the game board in a Nine Men's Morris game.

It handles the visual components of the board, including drawing the board structure, placing and removing stones, highlighting potential moves, and responding to user interactions. This class forms the visual bridge between the game's logic and the user interface.

Siehe auch:

    initNodePositions()
    Initialize the Map with 24 Entries, with a specific position (Point) on the Board The points are necessary for Mouse listeners.

    Initialize the Array with 9 Stones, with a specific position (Point) on the Board The points are necessary for Mouse listeners.

    When GUI-Board is initialized, this method will be called automatically to draw the Lines, Nodes and Stones.

    Resets the state of the GUIBoard, clearing any selected nodes and removing the dragged stone.

  • Konstruktordetails

    • GUIBoard

      public GUIBoard()

  • Methodendetails

    • initNodePositions

      private void initNodePositions()
      Initialize the Map with 24 Entries, with a specific position (Point) on the Board The points are necessary for Mouse listeners.

    • initStoneSlots

      private void initStoneSlots()
      Initialize the Array with 9 Stones, with a specific position (Point) on the Board The points are necessary for Mouse listeners.

    • handleStonePickup

      private boolean handleStonePickup(MouseEvent e, PlayerEntity currentPlayer)
      Handles the logic for picking up a stone from the side of the board. Checks if the current player is trying to pick up their own stone.
      Parameter:
      e - The mouse event containing the click position.
      currentPlayer - The player who is currently taking their turn.
      Gibt zurück:
      true if a stone was successfully picked up, false otherwise.

    • handleNodeSelection

      private void handleNodeSelection(MouseEvent e, PlayerEntity currentPlayer)
      Handles the logic for selecting a node on the board. Checks if the node has been clicked and gets it if so. Not necessary for the setting phase.
      Parameter:
      e - The mouse event containing the click position.
      currentPlayer - The player who is currently taking their turn.

    • tryExecutePlayerMove

      private void tryExecutePlayerMove(MouseEvent e)
      Attempts to execute a player move by checking if the mouse was released over a valid node. If a valid node is found, it signals the player move.
      Parameter:
      e - The mouse event containing the release position.

    • setMoveListener

      public void setMoveListener(Consumer<Node[]> listener)
      Sets a listener that will be notified when a complete move (from and to node) is selected.
      Parameter:
      listener - A consumer that accepts a Node array of length 2: [fromNode, toNode].

    • signalPlayerMove

      public void signalPlayerMove(Node from, Node to)
      Signals that the player has selected a move. This should be called once both mousePressed and mouseReleased have been processed.
      Parameter:
      from - The node the stone is moved from.
      to - The node the stone is moved to.

    • setRemoveListener

      public void setRemoveListener(Consumer<Node> listener)
      Sets a listener that will be notified when a complete move (remove) is selected.
      Parameter:
      listener - A consumer that accepts a Node to remove its stone.

    • setRemoveMode

      public void setRemoveMode(boolean toState)
      Changes the remove mode to a desired state. Can be used to activate or deactivate the remove mode. When the remove mode is active, a player can remove a stone by clicking on it.
      Parameter:
      toState - The target state of the removeMode.

      True: Remove mode is on. False: Remove mode is off.

    • getRemoveMode

      public boolean getRemoveMode()
      Returns the current state of the remove mode.
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the remove mode is active, false otherwise.

    • signalPlayerRemove

      public void signalPlayerRemove(Point targetPoint)
      When a mill is formed the player chooses one stone over the gui
      Parameter:
      targetPoint - - The Point(Stone) the Player wants to remove

    • paintComponent

      protected void paintComponent(Graphics g)
      When GUI-Board is initialized, this method will be called automatically to draw the Lines, Nodes and Stones. Can also be manually called by Component.repaint()
      Setzt außer Kraft:
      paintComponent in Klasse JComponent
      Parameter:
      g - the Graphics object
      Siehe auch:
      • drawBoardFrame(Graphics2D)
      • drawNodes(Graphics2D)
      • drawPlacedStones(Graphics2D)
      • drawStonePlaceholders(Graphics2D)
      • drawRemainingStones(Graphics2D)
      • drawDraggedStone(Graphics2D)

    • drawBoardFrame

      private void drawBoardFrame(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws the frame of the board with lines and rectangles.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.

    • drawNodes

      private void drawNodes(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws the nodes on the board based on their positions.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.

    • drawPlacedStones

      private void drawPlacedStones(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws the placed stones on the board, including their borders and filling colors.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.

    • drawStonePlaceholders

      private void drawStonePlaceholders(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws placeholders for stones that can be placed on the board. These are small circles indicating where stones can be displayed.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.

    • drawRemainingStones

      private void drawRemainingStones(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws the remaining stones that can be placed by each player. Displays how many stones are left for each player.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.
      Siehe auch:
      • drawReserveStones(Graphics2D, int, List, Color)

    • drawReserveStones

      private void drawReserveStones(Graphics2D g2, int stonesLeft, List<Point> stoneSlots, Color fillColor)
      Helper method for the drawRemainingStones(Graphics2D) method.

      It draws the stones that are left for each player in their respective slots.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.
      stonesLeft - The number of stones left for the player.
      stoneSlots - The list of points where the stones should be drawn.
      fillColor - The color to fill the stones with (white or black).

    • drawDraggedStone

      private void drawDraggedStone(Graphics2D g2)
      Helper method for the paintComponent(Graphics) method.

      Draws the stone that is currently being dragged by the player.

      Parameter:
      g2 - The Graphics2D object used for drawing.

    • resetState

      public void resetState()
      Resets the state of the GUIBoard, clearing any selected nodes and removing the dragged stone. This is useful for resetting the board after restarting a game.
